name: Retail Banking
frameworkNotes: OSFI B-13 requires vendor notification for cloud. Core system data must reside in Canada unless OSFI-approved.

employersByProvince.Ontario: RBC, TD Bank, Scotiabank, BMO, CIBC, Equitable Bank (Toronto)

entry: No security clearance required; standard criminal background check
AWS/Azure Certified Solutions Architect valued by Big 5
CISSP or CISM for security-adjacent roles
French-English bilingual valued at Desjardins, National Bank, Laurentian
Entry difficulty 4 = interview bar is high (system design), not role scarcity

name: Investment Banking
frameworkNotes: CIRO Rule 3200 data segregation. MNPI firewall enforcement between trading desks. Trading systems require sub-50ms latency.

keySecurityRequirements: audit trail for all trades and communications

name: Life Insurance
frameworkNotes: Health data triggers PHIPA (ON) or provincial equivalent. OSFI Guideline E-21 for operational risk. Federal insurers under OSFI; provincial under provincial acts.

name: P&C Insurance
frameworkNotes: Auto insurance publicly administered in BC (ICBC), MB (MPI), SK (SGI), QC (SAAQ) — separate regulatory regime. IFRS 17 financial reporting compliance.

name: Asset Management
frameworkNotes: NI 81-102 governs mutual funds. ETFs under NI 41-101. NI 31-103 registration required. Fund accounting systems require 99.9% uptime SLA.

name: Fintech & Neobanks
frameworkNotes: Canada Open Banking framework: federal progress disrupted by April 2025 election; legislative timeline under review. OSFI B-13 applies to tech-heavy operations. FINTRAC MSB registration mandatory for money services businesses.

name: Credit Unions
frameworkNotes: Provincially regulated only (no federal charter). Desjardins Group (QC) is Canada's largest credit union network. Deposit insurance varies by province.

name: Payments Processing
frameworkNotes: Payments Canada Modernization: Lynx RTGS system live, RTR real-time rail programme restructured (2024) — launch timeline under review. Interac dominant domestic network. ISO 20022 migration underway.

name: Hospitals & Health Systems
frameworkNotes: Data must stay within the province for patient records. PHIPA (ON) most stringent provincial law. Federal hospitals (Veterans Affairs, DND) follow PIPEDA additionally.

keySecurityRequirements: PHI encryption at rest and in transit
access controls per circle of care

name: Provincial Health Insurance
mandatoryCompliance: Provincial health insurance acts (OHIP-ON, MSP-BC, AHCIP-AB)
frameworkNotes: Each province operates own health insurance program. Data residency within province mandatory. Federal transfers via Canada Health Act. Billing systems require HL7/FHIR integration security.

name: Pharmaceutical
frameworkNotes: Health Canada requires 21 CFR Part 11-equivalent electronic records validation. CDSA for controlled substance tracking. GCP/GMP validation requirements affect CI/CD pipeline (change control procedures).

keySecurityRequirements: chain of custody for controlled substances

name: Biotech & Life Sciences Research
frameworkNotes: Genomic data requires explicit consent under TCPS 2 Article 13. ELSI (Ethical, Legal, Social Implications) framework applies. National Security Guidelines for Research Partnerships (2023) affect foreign-funded research.

keySecurityRequirements: research data sovereignty (data stays in Canada)

name: Medical Devices
frameworkNotes: Health Canada Class I–IV determines regulatory pathway. Class III/IV require QMS. SaMD (Software as Medical Device) follows IMDRF guidance. SBOM required for Class III/IV devices.

auditRequirements: Design history file: product lifetime + 10 yr

name: Clinical Diagnostics & Labs
frameworkNotes: LIS (Laboratory Information System) integration requires HL7 v2/FHIR security. Result sign-off workflow integrity mandatory. POCT (Point-of-Care Testing) devices follow specific Health Canada regulations.

keySecurityRequirements: access controls by ordering physician circle of care

name: Telehealth & Digital Health
regulators: Provincial colleges of regulated health professionals
frameworkNotes: Canadian data centres required for patient video/audio. Provincial practitioner licensing restricts cross-provincial care delivery. CASL applies to appointment reminders.

name: Long-term Care & Home Care
regulators: Provincial ministries of health and long-term care
frameworkNotes: IoT/smart home integration for resident monitoring requires additional security controls. Mobile care worker apps require MDM. Data sharing with acute care follows provincial health information custodian rules.

name: Federal Government
frameworkNotes: GC PBMM mandatory for cloud workloads. CCCS ITSG-33 IT security controls. Data residency in Canada mandatory. PSPC cloud procurement rules apply. ATO (Authority to Operate) process required.

keySecurityRequirements: identity via GCKey or Sign-In Canada

employersByProvince.Ontario: SSC, CRA, ESDC, IRCC, DND CIO, PSPC, Statistics Canada (Ottawa NCR)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: CRA Pacific, DFO, CBSA Pacific, Pacific Region digital (Victoria/Vancouver)
employersByProvince.Quebec: CBC/Radio-Canada tech, NRC, CFIA, IRB Quebec (Montréal/Quebec City)
employersByProvince.Alberta: Service Canada Alberta, CBSA Prairie Region (Edmonton)

entry: Reliability Status clearance minimum for all federal IT roles
Secret clearance required for approximately 40% of federal IT positions
Top Secret required for DND, CSE, and CSIS-adjacent roles
Canadian citizenship required for Secret clearance and above
Permanent Residents eligible for Reliability Status only
GC Cloud experience (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government) increasingly valued

name: Provincial Government
frameworkNotes: Data residency within province mandatory for most provincial systems. BC Cloud Hosting Services, Ontario Government cloud policy vary. Identity via provincial SSO.

keySecurityRequirements: Access controls per provincial RBAC policies

employersByProvince.Ontario: Ontario Digital Service, ServiceOntario, MGCS, OPS tech (Toronto/Ottawa)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: BC Digital Service (Victoria), OCIO BC, Ministry of Health IT
employersByProvince.Alberta: Service Alberta, Alberta Digital, ASIRT tech (Edmonton)
employersByProvince.New Brunswick: Service New Brunswick (Fredericton — shared services for Atlantic provinces)

entry: Provincial security clearance (Reliability Status equivalent) for most roles
No clearance required for some contracted roles through provincial tech procurement
Entry difficulty 3 = stable hiring; BC Digital is particularly active
Public sector pay grids cap salaries below private sector; compensated by stability
Service New Brunswick is a shared services model hiring for multiple Atlantic provinces

name: Municipal Government
frameworkNotes: Smart city deployments require Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs). IoT/sensor data in public spaces regulated by provincial FIPPA. Procurement through municipal procurement offices.

keySecurityRequirements: open data privacy review before publication

name: Crown Corporations
frameworkNotes: Each Crown operates under its own mandate act (CBC Act, Canada Post Act, VIA Rail). Commercial activities follow PIPEDA; government activities follow Privacy Act. Canada Post is critical infrastructure designated.

employersByProvince.Ontario: Canada Post HQ, CBC English digital, EDC, BDC, Via Rail IT (Ottawa/Toronto)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: Canada Post Pacific, NAV CANADA Pacific (Vancouver)
employersByProvince.Alberta: NAV CANADA Prairie, Canada Post Prairie ops (Calgary)

entry: Reliability Status clearance typically required
Entry difficulty 3 = moderate; stable hiring across Crown Corporations
Canada Post is a large tech employer — 500+ IT staff in Ottawa HQ
CBC/Radio-Canada requires bilingual candidates for all Montréal-based roles
NAV CANADA roles require Transport Canada security clearance

name: Public Safety & Law Enforcement
frameworkNotes: RCMP CPIC system requires CCCS approval. Provincial police systems (OPP, SQ) follow provincial data governance. Biometric data (fingerprints, DNA) under Privacy Act with additional restrictions.

keySecurityRequirements: intercept capability per Criminal Code Part VI

name: Justice & Courts
frameworkNotes: Electronic court filing systems (eCourt, Caselines) require strict access controls. Publication bans enforced at data access layer. Federal courts follow CAS IT standards; provincial courts follow provincial systems.

keySecurityRequirements: Publication ban enforcement in court record systems

name: Canadian Armed Forces
frameworkNotes: Classified networks (SNET/DWAN) separated from unclassified. COMSEC device approval by CSE. NATO interoperability requirements. ITSG-33 mandatory. Five Eyes data sharing protocols apply.

keySecurityRequirements: supply chain integrity for weapons systems

name: CSE (Signals Intelligence)
frameworkNotes: Most requirements classified. Public-facing CCCS cloud security guidance published. CCCS vulnerability disclosure coordination. CCCS Annual Threat Report governs GC cyber posture.

name: CSIS (Domestic Intelligence)
frameworkNotes: HUMINT source data has absolute legal protection. Warrant-based collection under CSIS Act. NSIRA oversight. Intelligence community data sharing via classified channels.

auditRequirements: warrant records sealed by court order

name: Defense Contractors
frameworkNotes: PSPC ISP mandatory for all defense contracts. Controlled Goods Program registration required. ITAR compliance for US-origin defense tech. PSPC requires facility security clearance. CITP (Canadian IT security profile) for system classification.

name: DRDC (Defence Research)
frameworkNotes: Research facilities hold facility security clearances. Quantum computing research has additional controls. Export controls on dual-use research. University collaborations require Tech Transfer Agreements.

name: Oil & Gas (Upstream)
frameworkNotes: OT (Operational Technology) networks strictly separated from IT. SCADA security per IEC 62443. Wellbore data subject to provincial Crown royalty reporting. Alberta is Canada's primary upstream hub.

employersByProvince.Alberta: Suncor, CNRL, Cenovus, Imperial Oil, MEG Energy, Baytex (Calgary T1)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: Tourmaline Oil, ARC Resources (Montney play — NE BC)
employersByProvince.Newfoundland: Cenovus Hibernia, ExxonMobil Hebron (St. John's offshore)

entry: No security clearance required
P.Eng (APEGA) valued for control systems and process engineering-adjacent roles
NERC CIP cybersecurity training (SANS ICS/SCADA) opens OT security roles
Entry difficulty 3 = Calgary accessible for mid-level DevOps and OT integration engineers
OSIsoft PI (AVEVA) experience is near-universal requirement for data engineering roles

name: Pipelines & Midstream
frameworkNotes: CER Critical Energy Infrastructure protection program. Trans Mountain, Enbridge, TC Energy pipelines under federal oversight. SCADA/DCS systems on isolated OT networks. Geospatial pipeline route data is sensitive.

keySecurityRequirements: cross-border data sharing with US NERC

name: Electricity & Utilities
frameworkNotes: NERC CIP mandatory for bulk electric systems connected to North American grid. Smart meter data is PII under PIPEDA. Ontario IESO and BC Hydro operate under distinct provincial frameworks.

keySecurityRequirements: NERC CIP CIP-002 through CIP-013 controls
auditRequirements: Smart meter data varies by province

name: Nuclear Energy
mandatoryCompliance: Security of Information Act (national security aspects)
frameworkNotes: CNSC regulates all Canadian nuclear facilities. Bruce Power, OPG (ON), NB Power Point Lepreau, AECL/CNL. Safety systems on air-gapped networks. Nuclear material accounting reported to IAEA. CANDU reactor-specific cybersecurity requirements.

name: Renewables & Cleantech
frameworkNotes: Grid interconnection requires NERC CIP compliance for bulk systems. Smart inverter protocols (IEEE 2030.5) security requirements. Carbon credit systems subject to PIPEDA and provincial regulations. IESO/AESO market data feeds require secure access.

name: Mining & Resources
frameworkNotes: Geological survey data (seismic, assay) is commercially sensitive IP. TSX/TSX-V material information disclosure rules. Indigenous consultation records may require privacy protections. Automated mine systems follow IEC 62443.

auditRequirements: Geological data retained as trade secret

employersByProvince.British Columbia: Teck Resources HQ (Vancouver), Goldcorp/Newmont BC, Glencore Canada
employersByProvince.Ontario: Barrick Gold (Toronto HQ), Agnico Eagle (Toronto), Kinross HQ (Toronto)
employersByProvince.Quebec: Agnico Eagle LaRonde mine digital, Osisko Mining (Montréal HQ)

entry: No security clearance required
P.Eng or Geoscientist (EGBC, APEGA) valued for mine planning and geotechnical roles
Autonomous systems experience (ROS, Komatsu FrontRunner SDK) is fastest-growing niche
Entry difficulty 3 = niche but steady; remote site work common in BC and Northern Ontario
Rotational work common — 14/14, 20/10 schedules; premium pay for remote assignments

name: Software & SaaS
frameworkNotes: CASL applies to all commercial electronic messages to Canadian addresses. Quebec Law 25 is stricter than PIPEDA. GC customers require GC PBMM compliance.

name: AI & ML Platforms
frameworkNotes: AIDA (Bill C-27) lapsed when Parliament dissolved April 2025 — not in force as of May 2026. TBS Directive on Automated Decision-Making applies to federal government AI. Biometric data in training sets has heightened requirements.

keySecurityRequirements: consent for personal data in training

employersByProvince.Ontario: Cohere (Toronto), Layer 6 (TD AI), NVIDIA Toronto lab, Google DeepMind Toronto, Microsoft AI Toronto
employersByProvince.Quebec: Mila-affiliated startups, Element AI legacy (ServiceNow), Scale AI secretariat (Montréal)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: D-Wave Systems (Burnaby), Accenture AI, Amazon Science Vancouver
employersByProvince.Ontario/Quebec: Vector Institute affiliates (UofT, McGill, Waterloo researchers)

entry: No security clearance required for commercial AI roles
PhD or MSc valued but not mandatory — strong GitHub portfolio with ML projects accepted
Vector Institute, Mila, Amii research network connections accelerate hiring
Entry difficulty 4 = competitive, not credential-locked; research vs product pedigree split
Cohere and D-Wave are the most accessible large employers for ML infrastructure roles

name: Cloud Services
frameworkNotes: AWS Canada (Central and West), Azure Canada Central/East, GCP Canada provide Canadian data residency. GC Marketplace for PSPC-approved cloud services. CCCS ITSP.50.105 cloud security guidance. GC PBMM required for all federal workloads.

name: Cybersecurity
frameworkNotes: CCCS (Canadian Centre for Cyber Security) is primary guidance body. GC clients require ITSG-33. CCCS Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure guidelines for security research. CCCS Annual Threat Assessment governs national posture.

employersByProvince.Ontario: BlackBerry Security (Waterloo/Toronto), eSentire (Cambridge), Arctic Wolf CA, Cyclera (Toronto)

entry: No security clearance for commercial roles; Reliability Status for GC contracts
OSCP, GREM, or GCIH certifications valued for threat intel and IR roles
Entry difficulty 4 = advanced technical skill required
BlackBerry (Waterloo) is Canada's largest cybersecurity product employer
Bug bounty track record and open source security tools are accepted as portfolio

name: Hardware & Semiconductor
frameworkNotes: Canada's National Quantum Strategy (2022) includes security implications. ITAR/EAR applies to exported goods containing US technology. Quantum hardware research at IQC (Waterloo) and TRIUMF.

auditRequirements: Design IP records retained as trade secret

name: Wireless Carriers (MNO)
frameworkNotes: Big 3: Bell, Rogers, Telus. CRTC-mandated MVNO wholesale access (2021 decision). Lawful interception under Criminal Code Part VI. 5G security guidance from CCCS (ITSAP.80.004). ISED manages spectrum allocation.

name: Internet Service Providers
frameworkNotes: CRTC net neutrality rules (Internet Traffic Management Practices). Mandatory DMARC/DNSSEC deployment guidance. Domain blocking orders (piracy via court order). CASL requires cooperation with CRTC enforcement.

auditRequirements: Traffic logs 1 yr (court order may extend)

name: Broadcasting & Cable
frameworkNotes: Online Streaming Act (C-11, 2023) extends CRTC jurisdiction to streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney+). CanCon requirements mandate % of Canadian programming. National Public Alerting System (NPAS) emergency broadcast mandatory.

name: Satellite Communications
frameworkNotes: Telesat, MDA Group key Canadian players. Radarsat/WCAAF earth observation data may be defense-adjacent. Telesat Lightspeed LEO constellation under development. Canadian Space Agency data sharing protocols.

name: Grocery & Food Retail
frameworkNotes: Loblaws, Metro, Sobeys, Costco. Loyalty programs (PC Optimum, Scene+) subject to PIPEDA consent. CFIA food safety traceability systems (STELA). PIPEDA breach notification if loyalty data compromised.

name: E-Commerce & Marketplace
frameworkNotes: Shopify (Waterloo/Ottawa origin), Amazon.ca, Kijiji. CASL consent mandatory for transactional email. Quebec Law 25 stricter consent requirements than federal PIPEDA. PIPEDA consent for behavioral tracking.

name: General Retail
frameworkNotes: Hudson's Bay Company, Canadian Tire, Dollarama. In-store facial recognition analytics banned under OPC/provincial orders. Quebec Law 25 applies to Quebec retail operations. Loyalty programs under PIPEDA.

name: Automotive Manufacturing
frameworkNotes: Ontario auto corridor: Windsor, Oshawa, Cambridge, Ingersoll. Stellantis, GM, Toyota, Honda plants. Connected vehicle data follows PIPEDA. Autonomous vehicle testing regulated by Transport Canada and Ontario.

name: Aerospace & Aviation
frameworkNotes: Bombardier, Pratt & Whitney Canada, Magellan Aerospace, CAE. DO-178C/DO-326A for avionics software certification. ITAR applies to US-origin aerospace components. Defense contracts require PSPC ISP. Montreal is Canada's aerospace hub.

keySecurityRequirements: ITAR export controls on tech transfer
auditRequirements: DO-178C qualification records retained with aircraft

name: Food Processing & Manufacturing
frameworkNotes: SFCR requires traceability one step back, one step forward. CFIA Preventive Control Plans (PCPs) must be in digital systems. McCain Foods, Maple Leaf Foods, Saputo are key anchors. Allergen management system integrity is critical.

name: Industrial Equipment & Heavy Mfg
frameworkNotes: Finning International, Toromont, CNH Industrial Canada. OT/SCADA for automated production lines. Predictive maintenance IoT creates data obligations. Export of dual-use equipment under Export Controls Order.

name: Broadcasting (TV & Radio)
frameworkNotes: CBC/Radio-Canada, CTV, Global, Corus, Bell Media. CanCon requirements per CRTC. National Public Alerting System (NPAS) emergency broadcast mandatory. French-language programming requirements for QC-distributed content.

name: Video Gaming
frameworkNotes: Montreal gaming hub: Ubisoft, Eidos-Montréal, WB Games, Square Enix. Quebec government game tax credits. Loot box regulation under review in Canada. Quebec Law 25 stricter for QC studios.

name: Film & TV Production
frameworkNotes: Vancouver (BC Film) and Toronto (Ontario Creates) primary hubs. Federal/provincial tax credits (CAVCO, OMDC) require Canadian content certification. MPA content security standards for major studio co-productions. ACTRA/IATSE union data management.

employersByProvince.British Columbia: ILM Vancouver, Deluxe BC, Pixomondo Vancouver, Image Engine (Vancouver)
employersByProvince.Ontario: Technicolor Toronto, Industrial Brothers, Guru Studio (Toronto)
employersByProvince.Quebec: Rodeo FX (Montréal), Framestore Montréal, Lakeside Studio (Montréal)

entry: No security clearance required
Houdini + Python pipeline scripting is the key hire signal for TD (Technical Director) roles
Entry difficulty 3 = junior compositor and TD roles available; Vancouver is Canada's VFX hub
Tax credits make Vancouver and Montréal dominant; Toronto is smaller by comparison
BCIT Film + Humber animation are the major Canadian feeder programs

name: Digital Media & Publishing
frameworkNotes: Online News Act (C-18, 2023) governs Google/Meta payments to news publishers. CASL consent for newsletters and notifications. Copyright Act AI training data exemptions under review. Quebec Law 25 cookie consent requirements.

auditRequirements: Subscriber data 3 yr after subscription ends

employersByProvince.Ontario: Postmedia (Toronto), Globe and Mail (Toronto), TorStar (Toronto), Maclean's/Rogers Media
employersByProvince.Quebec: Quebecor/Canoe Media (Montréal), Le Devoir, La Presse digital (Montréal)

entry: No security clearance required
Demand is declining overall; subscription tech is the growth pocket within a contracting sector
Entry difficulty 3 = accessible but sector is contracting
Bill C-18 has created unexpected demand for compliance tooling and negotiation support
React/Next.js + SEO knowledge is the core skill for front-end publishing roles

name: Universities & Research
frameworkNotes: Tri-Agency Research Data Management Policy requires data management plans. National Security Guidelines for Research Partnerships (2023) restrict some foreign-funded research. NSERC restrictions apply to sensitive research areas.

keySecurityRequirements: national security vetting for research partnerships (NSICOP guidance)
auditRequirements: Research data per RDM plan (typically 10 yr)

name: K-12 Schools
frameworkNotes: Ontario school boards under MFIPPA. BC under FOIPPA. US-hosted platforms (Google Workspace for Education, Microsoft 365) under OPC scrutiny. Ed-tech vendor reviews required. Indigenous student data sovereignty per OCAP® principles.

keySecurityRequirements: cloud vendor data residency for student data

name: Online Learning & EdTech
frameworkNotes: D2L (Desire2Learn — Waterloo), Top Hat, Prodigy are Canadian EdTech anchors. CASL consent for course reminders. AODA accessibility compliance mandatory in Ontario. Quebec Law 25 cookie consent for QC learners.

keySecurityRequirements: payment card security for course fees

employersByProvince.Ontario: D2L HQ (Kitchener), Top Hat (Toronto), Riipen (Toronto reg.), Prodigy Education (Toronto)
employersByProvince.Alberta: Apply to Education (Calgary), Alberta Digital Learning Alliance (Edmonton)

entry: No security clearance required
Entry difficulty 3 = growing sector with active junior hiring at D2L and Top Hat
LTI and xAPI knowledge opens LMS integration roles
D2L (Kitchener) is the most accessible large EdTech employer in Canada
TypeScript + React + accessibility (WCAG) is the front-end skill stack that wins EdTech roles

name: Air Transport
frameworkNotes: Air Canada, WestJet, Transat. CATSA for passenger screening. API/PNR data shared with CBSA under CANPASS. Nav Canada air traffic control systems are critical infrastructure. ICAO cybersecurity standards (Doc 10164).

name: Rail Transport
frameworkNotes: CN, CP (CPKC), VIA Rail, commuter rail (GO Transit, RTM). SCADA for wayside signaling and PTC (Positive Train Control). TDG Act for dangerous goods manifests. Critical infrastructure designation for transcontinental rail.

name: Trucking & Logistics
frameworkNotes: ELD (Electronic Logging Device) mandate (2021) creates regulated driver data. CBSA eManifest for cross-border shipments. C-TPAT for Canada-US border trade. PIP (Partners in Protection) program for trusted shippers.

keySecurityRequirements: ELD data protection (driver hours and location)

name: Marine, Ports & Shipping
frameworkNotes: Port authorities (Vancouver, Montreal, Halifax, Prince Rupert) designated critical infrastructure. ISPS Code security plans are classified. VTS (Vessel Traffic Services) under Nav Canada/Coast Guard. Arctic sovereignty implications for Northern shipping routes.

name: Urban & Public Transit
frameworkNotes: Presto (Metrolinx-ON), Compass (TransLink-BC), Opus (STM-QC). Tap-in/tap-out creates detailed location history under PIPEDA. AODA accessibility compliance mandatory for Ontario transit apps. TTC, OC Transpo, STM, Calgary Transit, Edmonton Transit.

name: Residential Real Estate
mandatoryCompliance: Prohibition on Purchase by Non-Canadians Act (2022)
frameworkNotes: FINTRAC requires realtors to collect client ID and beneficial ownership (since 2023 amendments). Foreign buyer ban under Prohibition on Purchase by Non-Canadians Act (2022). BC Beneficial Ownership Land Title Registry (LOTR) integration required.

employersByProvince.Ontario: Properly (Toronto), Ratehub (Toronto), Centract (Toronto), Royal LePage Digital (Mississauga)
employersByProvince.British Columbia: Doormat (Vancouver), Sutton Group digital, RE/MAX BC digital
employersByProvince.Quebec: Via Capitale digital, Century 21 QC portal (Montréal)

entry: No security clearance required
Entry difficulty 3 = niche; CREA DDF knowledge differentiates candidates
CREA Data Distribution Facility (DDF) API expertise is the specialist credential
Ratehub and Properly are the most accessible technology-focused residential real estate employers
React + mapping (Mapbox GL/Leaflet) is the dominant frontend skill pair for listing portals

name: Commercial Real Estate
mandatoryCompliance: Provincial real estate and planning acts
frameworkNotes: Cadillac Fairview, Oxford Properties, RioCan. Building automation systems (BAS) require OT security. REIT disclosure rules under CSA National Instruments. Smart building IoT creates new PIPEDA obligations.

name: Property Management
frameworkNotes: Boardwalk, Killam, Minto. Tenant credit checks under provincial tenancy acts. Smart lock and access fob data creates location PII under PIPEDA. Maintenance request data is PII. FINTRAC applies for cash rental transactions.

auditRequirements: Tenancy records 3 yr after tenancy ends

name: Crop Farming & Grain
mandatoryCompliance: CFIA (Feeds Act, Seeds Act, Plant Protection Act)
frameworkNotes: Canadian Grain Commission regulates grain grading and weighing. Prairie grain belt (SK, MB, AB) is primary region. Precision agriculture platforms (Farmers Edge, Trimble) handle sensitive crop yield/soil data. Provincial farm data legislation emerging (Alberta leading).

name: Livestock & Aquaculture
frameworkNotes: RFID animal traceability via CCIA (Canadian Cattle Identification Agency) for bovine. DFO aquaculture licensing for BC salmon and shellfish. Avian influenza/disease outbreak reporting is mandatory. HACCP plans must be in digital systems.

name: AgriTech & Precision Ag
frameworkNotes: Farmers Edge, Decisive Farming, Veritas Farm Management are key Canadian AgriTech firms. Farm data ownership legislation has no federal law yet (Alberta leading with emerging rules). Satellite/drone imagery creates data sovereignty questions. Saskatoon is Canada's AgriTech hub.

keySecurityRequirements: crop yield prediction model IP protection

name: Law Firms
mandatoryCompliance: Provincial law society rules (LSO-ON, LSBC, BSQ-QC)
frameworkNotes: Solicitor-client privilege creates absolute confidentiality obligation. Law Society of Ontario Technology Practice Area guidelines. FINTRAC reporting for real estate lawyers and trust accounts. Cloud storage requires law society compliance assessment before adoption.

auditRequirements: File retention per law society rules (typically 10 yr)

name: Accounting, Audit & Tax
frameworkNotes: Big 4 Canadian practices (Deloitte, PwC, KPMG, EY). CSQM (Canadian Standard on Quality Management) for firms. CRA e-filing security (EFILE). PCAOB oversight for US-listed Canadian companies. Data analytics in audit creates bulk financial data obligations.

name: Management Consulting
frameworkNotes: McKinsey, Deloitte, PwC, Accenture, KPMG Canadian practices. Government consulting contracts require PSPC security screening (reliability or secret). No sector-specific regulation — inherits client regulatory context.

name: Registered Charities
frameworkNotes: CRA charity registration requires annual T3010 reporting. CASL rules for non-profits relaxed but commercial electronic message rules still apply for fundraising. International fund transfer reporting if political activities exceed 10% threshold.

auditRequirements: Donor PII 7 yr post last donation

name: Healthcare Charities
frameworkNotes: Canadian Cancer Society, Heart & Stroke, MS Society. Patient data subject to PHIPA where circle-of-care adjacent. Research funding requires TCPS 2 alignment. Patient registries under PHIPA. CRA charity rules limit advocacy activities.

keySecurityRequirements: Patient PII/PHI segmentation from donor data

name: Indigenous Community Services
frameworkNotes: OCAP® principles: data must be Owned and Controlled by the community. FNIGC (First Nations Information Governance Centre) governs standards. Sixties Scoop and residential school records require special handling. ISC funding agreements require data sharing with consent.

auditRequirements: Band council records per community governance
